closing_text: Career Opportunities: Horticultural Specialist (15097) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Horticultural Specialist (15097) Requisition ID 15097 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Cultivate your career while caring for Boroondara’s parks and gardens Full-time, ongoing opportunity located in Kew $71,312 to $76,789 excl super plus 9-day fortnight If you’ve worked in local government, you know that not all Parks & Gardens teams are created equal. About Boroondara At the City of Boroondara, we are committed to creating a sustainable and inclusive community. We are seeking a dedicated Horticultural Specialist to join our Open Space and Trees team. This is an opportunity to help care for some of Melbourne’s most valued parks, gardens and streetscapes while contributing to a high‐performing and customer‐focused organisation. About the role In this role, you will support the development, care and ongoing maintenance of Council’s parks, formal gardens, streetscapes, carparks and open spaces. You will apply best‐practice horticultural techniques to ensure our green spaces remain healthy, attractive and welcoming for residents and visitors. The role involves working collaboratively with colleagues across Council and engaging respectfully with contractors, community representatives and members of the public. Key responsibilities You will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing horticultural assets using high‐quality horticultural practices. You will regularly assess and prioritise work across multiple sites and ensure all areas meet required standards and maintenance schedules. The role includes monitoring and operating irrigation systems, conducting site audits, identifying redevelopment and planting opportunities, and maintaining minor plant and equipment. You will also uphold safe work procedures and contribute to creating a safe, well‐managed workplace. About you Here, you’ll be entrusted with your own geographic patch – a chance to take ownership, make decisions, and see your work come to life. You’ll be supported by a team that values skill, initiative, and collaboration, and you’ll enjoy the satisfaction of working on some of the most prestigious gardens in the country. This is your opportunity to work in a team that’s proud of what it does and where your contribution will be visible every day – in gardens that are the envy of Melbourne. Want to know more? Please refer to the position description for more information. Employment requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check, a Working with Children check and relevant pre-employment medical assessments How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11.59pm, Sunday 7 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Simon Jones , Acting Coordinator Parks, Environmental Sustainability & Open Spaces on 0466 544 866 during business hours or simon.woods@boroondara.vic.gov.au I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal - we value your input and look forward to our journey ahead. #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) Requisition ID 15080 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Local Laws Officer Join a dynamic, investigations team that supports each other to succeed Deliver meaningful outcomes to the community in safety and amenity $91,334 - $98,476 per annum (plus Super) Tool of trade vehicle provided (non-hybrid position) Join us in building a professional investigations team and help deliver a responsive, community-focused Local Laws service. In this role, you will contribute to a safe, liveable and well-maintained municipality while providing high-quality regulatory service to residents and stakeholders. About the role At City of Boroondara, our Local Laws directly contributes to our purpose of protecting the health, safety, amenity and assets of the municipality. Based within the large and busy department of Civic Services, the team works as part of five field-based teams and many others within the Urban Living Directorate. You will respond to reported breaches of the Amenity Local Law and the Environment Protection Act, using education, investigation and enforcement to achieve compliance. The role involves managing a varied caseload, gathering evidence, preparing clear records and working within established procedures to resolve matters fairly and effectively. The position includes investigating issues such as illegally dumped rubbish under the EPA, unsightly properties, overhanging vegetation, illegally advertising and the occupation of Council land, while providing courteous and timely service to community members. From time to time, you will also support the Animal Management team with park patrols, and work with external stakeholders including Victoria Police, the Magistrates’ Court and Council prosecutors. Hours are allocated over a 16-week roster (42-hours per week) and rotating weekend work is required every 4 weeks with penalty rates that convert into an annualised salary. A fully maintained vehicle is provided as a ‘tool of trade’ to perform necessary duties and commute to and from work. This is a non-hybrid role as our ‘office’ is in the community, every day and our success comes from working together with other officers, as a team! About you We are looking for someone who brings sound judgement, a strong customer focus and a collaborative approach. You will be supported to build your knowledge and succeed in the role. You will have experience delivering customer service in a complex regulatory environment or another fast-paced setting, with the ability to communicate clearly, build rapport and respond professionally in a range of situations under pressure. Experience in areas such as retail, hospitality, community services or other enforcement role can provide a strong foundation for success in this role. You will be able to investigate issues methodically, collect information accurately and prepare clear notes and reports. Knowledge of compliance, local laws or related legislation will be well regarded, as will the ability to interpret policy and apply it in practical situations. If you are still developing in these areas, a willingness to learn will be equally valued. Success in this role also requires respectful negotiation skills, sound problem-solving and judgement skills, de-escalating interpersonal situations with members of the community, and the capacity to work across the community each day, including walking for extended periods and assisting with animal handling when required. A qualification in enforcement, local laws, justice or a related field is desirable, but not essential. Want to know more? Please refer to the Position description What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development You will be part of a team that makes a real difference. Working at Boroondara means contributing to creating positive change in the world. Join us in a role where your work matters, and you contribute to meaningful outcomes. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check. All City of Boroondara employees must be fully vaccinated. Appointment of the successful candidate will be subject to provision of evidence of vaccination status or medical exemption prior to commencement. How to Apply Follow these steps, carefully: Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply ’ button by Sunday 31 May 2026 11:59pm . Ensure you address your cover letter to Natasha Curmi and answer the following questions: What about the Local Laws Officer role motivates you? Why is this the next logical step in your career? All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any confidential discussion please contact Natasha Curmi, Team Leader Compliance (during business hours) on 0459 854 662 . We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. commencing $61,425 pa (pro-rata) / $31.09 per hour + Super + Allowances Casual - $38.86 per hour (incl. loading) + Super + Allowances Closing Date: Applications are accepted at all times. We are seeking skilled and experienced professional care workers who love supporting people to live independently in the community. We currently provide services in Mount Alexander Shire and Central Goldfields Shire. This is your opportunity to bring your passion and skills to be part of a professional and dedicated team providing high quality in-home support to frail older people and people with disabilities in our community. The role provides tailored services to help clients live healthy, independent and active lives through assistance with daily tasks and activities. If you have any queries or would like to discuss the role, the key contact is: Sarah Newstead In Home Support Coordinator (03) 5471 1845 Please note these positions require staff to be triple vaccinated (2 doses plus a booster). To apply, please provide a cover letter (no more than 2 pages) outlining why you are interested and the best candidate for this role, and your current resume. Mount Alexander Shire Council is committed to child safety. We have zero tolerance of child abuse. Our robust human resources, recruitment and vetting practices are strictly adhered to during the application and interviewing process. Applicants should be aware that we conduct working with children, police and reference checks to ensure that we are recruiting the right people. Apply now Email this job Back to top Acknowledgement of Country Mount Alexander Shire Council acknowledges that the Traditional Custodians of this land, the Dja Dja Wurrung and Taungurung peoples, proudly survive. We acknowledge their continued practise of custom and their close cultural, spiritual, physical, social, historical and economic relationship with the land and waters that make up their Country, which includes Mount Alexander Shire. school crossing supervisor Listen Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Print Content components School crossing supervisors have a much-valued role in Boroondara. On an average school day in Boroondara, they help 9,266 pedestrians to cross the street while 74,426 motor vehicles use our roads during school crossing times. We are always looking for school crossing supervisors. If you are friendly, reliable and flexible, this role is for you. What does a school crossing supervisor do? This paid position gives you the opportunity to get involved in your community and improve road safety for children, people with a disability and the general public who use our 114 school crossings. School crossing supervisors: • work part-time Monday to Friday, from 8 am to 9 am and from 3 pm to 4 pm, during the school term • play an important role in educating children with ‘Stop, Look and Listen’ instructions • are responsible for reporting any road hazards or potential risks • help to encourage active and independent travel. About you We are looking for people aged 18 and older who: • can attend shifts at short notice • have their own reliable transport • are able to work across several locations • can complete the pre-employment medical and police checks. You will have access to a full training program before starting your role. Download the position description School Crossing Supervisor PD 144.04 KB [PDF] 23 November 2022 How to apply Learn more about this rewarding role by reading the position description above. Submit an online application through our Careers website . Attend an interview – meet with one of our Council Officers at Council, located at 8 Inglesby Road in Camberwell. Provide us with relevant employment documents – including your Working With Children Check, Police Check and evidence of your COVID-19 vaccination status. Complete a medical assessment. Apply to become a School Crossings Supervisor We are always recruiting new school crossing supervisors to help people to cross our roads safely at peak school times. More information Call (03) 9278 4774 or email [email protected] for more information. Careers at Boroondara Learn more about what it's like to work with us. Learn more Was this page helpful? Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Senior Administration Officer (15102) Requisition ID 15102 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Lead the delivery of high-quality administration in a dynamic Council environment Ongoing, full-time opportunity based in Kew, flexible arrangements can be considered $84,770 - $98,205 (plus super and monthly RDO) About Boroondara The City of Boroondara is a vibrant, inclusive and thriving community in Melbourne’s inner east. Guided by our vision of a healthy, connected and sustainable community, we deliver services that place our customers at the centre of everything we do. Our people are collaborative, innovative and committed to making a difference for our community every day. About the Role We are seeking a highly organised and proactive Senior Administration Officer to provide professional and high-level support to the Manager Open Space and Trees and the whole department within the Places & Spaces Directorate. Based at Kew Depot, this role plays a key part in ensuring the smooth and effective operation of the department, while acting as a central coordination point for councillor and CEO correspondence. Working across a diverse range of stakeholders, you will deliver quality administrative support, coordinate key processes, and contribute to strong customer service outcomes that support our community and our Department purpose for a greener Boroondara. Key Responsibilities In this role, you will provide comprehensive administrative and organisational support to the Manager and Department, including document preparation, meeting coordination and office management activities. You will manage incoming and outgoing correspondence, ensuring responses are timely and accurate. You will support financial and purchasing processes, including raising purchase orders and maintaining departmental supplies, while ensuring adherence to Council procedures. The role also oversees records management, including monitoring systems and ensuring service standards and response timeframes are achieved. You will deliver high-quality customer service to a wide range of internal and external stakeholders and assist with resolving complex enquiries. Additionally, you will contribute to reporting, process improvements and documentation, maintaining efficient systems that support the department’s performance and compliance obligations. About You You are an experienced administration professional with a strong track record of supporting senior leaders in a complex organisation. You bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to prepare high-quality reports, correspondence and documentation. You are highly organised, detail-oriented and able to prioritise competing demands while meeting deadlines. Your advanced Microsoft Office skills and ability to learn new systems quickly enable you to work efficiently and effectively. With a proactive, solutions-focused mindset, you demonstrate initiative and sound judgement, particularly when dealing with sensitive matters. You thrive both independently and as part of a team, building strong relationships across all levels and delivering exceptional customer service. Please refer to the attached position description for more information. Employment Requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check and may also require a Working with Children Check. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11.59pm, Sunday 14 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential conversation please contact David Shepard , Manager, Open Space and Trees on 0409 913 482 or david.shepard@boroondara.vic.gov.au during business hours. I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal description_excerpt: Lead the delivery of high-quality administration in a dynamic Council environment Ongoing, full-time opportunity based in Kew, flexible arrangements can be considered $84,770 - $98,205 (plus super and monthly RDO) About Boroondara The City of Boroondara is a vibrant, inclusive and thriving...
title: Senior Administration Officer

advertised_salary_text: $84,770 - $98,205 per annum plus super and RDO We are seeking a capable and community-minded Insurance Offi
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Insurance Officer (15105) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Insurance Officer (15105) Requisition ID 15105 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Insurance Officer Join Council’s Governance and Legal Department Be part of a supportive and collaborative team Full-time, ongoing position $84,770 - $98,205 per annum plus super and RDO We are seeking a capable and community-minded Insurance Officer to join Council’s Governance and Legal Department. In this role, you will manage a broad range of insurance claims, contribute to risk and assurance activities, and help deliver fair, timely and well-informed outcomes for Council and the community. About you We are looking for someone who is passionate about providing outstanding service and making a positive impact in the community. You will bring: A strong understanding of insurance claims management and tort law principles, ideally within a government setting Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to write clear and accurate responses Strong attention to detail, helping you process claims thoroughly and on time A customer-focused approach and a collaborative mindset – you enjoy working with others to achieve the best outcomes Confidence using multiple systems and tools to manage your work efficiently Key responsibilities The primary role of the Insurance Officer is to process claims made against Council. Claims can include damage from Council trees and trucks, personal injury, water flow, and professional liability. You will investigate incidents, draft correspondence, and advocate for outcomes in the best interest of Council and the community. You will also manage some court claims, assisting Council’s insurer and their lawyers. Additionally, you will coordinate the discovery process for litigated claims and manage Freedom of Information requests. As part of the Risk and Assurance team within Council’s Governance and Legal Department, the role also assists with: Crisis management coordination Maintaining Council’s Business Continuity Plans Implementing and managing Council's Risk Management Framework and enterprise risk register Council’s insurance renewal program Fraud and corruption control What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships. Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by Sunday 7 June 2026, 11:59pm . All applications must be made via the online portal. For further information, please view the position description . For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Rudi Dellow on (03) 9278 4686 during business hours. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. With so much going on it’s a great time to join Boroondara! #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Volunteer Sector Development Officer (15092) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Volunteer Sector Development Officer (15092) Requisition ID 15092 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Volunteer Sector Development Officer Play a key role to promote and raise the profile of volunteering across Boroondara Full time, Fixed Term Contract until 30 June 2027. $84,770 to $98,205 Band 5 per annum plus Superannuation and RDO Make a difference in the community by developing activities that raise the profile of volunteering, develop good volunteer engagement practice, and support community strengthening, social inclusion and wellbeing objectives across Boroondara. A little bit about the role Working in a hybrid environment and reporting to the Volunteer Support & Development Lead, the Volunteer Sector Development Officer will be responsible for a portfolio of activities for which volunteers, volunteer-involving organisations, and Council departments are the primary audience or customer group. The role will also be responsible for, but not limited to: Working in collaboration with the Volunteer Support & Development Lead to develop initiatives which respond to current and emerging trends in volunteering Organising large events designed to connect prospective volunteers including professionals to community organisations as well as recognise and promote volunteering Building the capacity of volunteer-involving organisations, and Council departments to engage volunteers by organising workshops, forums or online resources Supporting internal Council volunteer management Ongoing development of the Boroondara Volunteer Skills Bank, and Ongoing development of the Boroondara volunteer management database A little bit about you You will have outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal, who is both personable and approachable to a range of audiences including all levels of local government, external organisations, networks, and the community. You will have a demonstrated understanding of the volunteer sector. You will have experience in project development and the delivery of large events. You will also have comprehensive computer skills across the Microsoft Office range of applications and a willingness to work as part of a team, prepared to share experiences with other team members to improve overall knowledge. The successful incumbent will also have: a qualification or continuing study in the fields of community development, social science or human services preferred a commitment to quality outcomes and efficient work practices, and excellent time management and organisation skills Employment Requirements Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check and hold a valid Working with Children Check. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply’ button by Wednesday 10 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Tara Cantwell (Acting Volunteer Support and Development Lead) on 9278 4763 or Caddy Purdy (Senior Coordinator Community Strengthening) on 9278 4869 during business hours for a confidential discussion. If you want to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our Careers Website. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some applicants may be required to hold a Working with Children Clearance. Additionally, some applicants may also be required to undergo a National Criminal History check and/or medical check as per the requirements of the role. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Transfer Station Attendant / Operator (15086) Requisition ID 15086 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Standard work week is Tuesday to Saturday Attractive remuneration, $86,472 + Superannuation Ongoing, full-time About Boroondara Boroondara is continuously evolving as part of our ongoing commitment to become a truly customer centric organisation. Through technology, innovation, collaboration and leadership, we are realigning how we work together to enhance the customer experience. An exciting opportunity has arisen to play a vital role supporting our Transforming Boroondara Program. About the role We are seeking an Attendant Operator to work in our Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team, which is located at the Boroondara Waste and Recycling Centre operated by the City of Boroondara. The team is responsible for the receiving, organising and cartage of hard, household, recyclable and organic waste. Our team members enjoy variety of work in a very busy operational work site which is open 7 days a week. If you have experience in the Waste industry - whether from private industry or councils, or you come from the building and earthmoving industries, we welcome your application. Key responsibilities of the role include: The morning is spent cleaning and preparing to receive council’s internal waste trucks and directing our drivers where to park for waste to be unloaded. In the afternoon the Transfer Station operates a public service, for the public to deposit their hard waste and recycling materials. This involves interacting with the public, traffic management and providing clear directions where to deposit waste such as organic matter, mattresses, tyres, steel, bricks etc. Throughout the day all waste is loaded onto trailers for cartage to landfill, sorting centres and mulching sites. All the Transfer Station and Yard Maintenance team members are trained in all tasks and are required to work on all duties , ranging from control room operation, various plant operations, yard maintenance and cleaning, assist in after-hours emergencies and vehicle maintenance. About you To be successful in this role you will bring: It is in your nature to be a productive team member and have a high work ethic. You will join a small and already cohesive team, and you have excellent interpersonal skills. You will use your OHS skills to ensure your work site is secure from hazards and is safe for you, your colleagues and the public, and promptly report hazards. You have current Licences for operating Backhoe, Forklift and Front-end Loader. If you recently received your Licences, we welcome your application. Additional information Your ordinary work week will be Tuesday to Saturday Your ordinary hours of work are 42.5 hours a week. Your workday is from 7am to 4pm. A 30-minute break is within this time. Overtime will be paid only if you work more than 42.5 hours a week. The advertised salary is inclusive of all Public Holidays which are worked. Public Holidays not worked are Good Friday, Christmas Day and New Years Day and are paid at normal time. ‘Make up’ days are prior to or after these 3 Public Holidays and are on a Saturday only. Appropriate overtime will be paid on these 3 days. The AFL Grand Final Parade Day is worked and will be paid at the appropriate overtime rates. Why Join us? You will join a great work environment which offers: a great team culture and a fair and supportive manager active learning and development opportunities prime office location in Camberwell with cafes, retail and restaurants nearby generous parental leave and other leave entitlements wellbeing programs, free flu vaccinations, free skin checks Want to know more? Check out the Position Description Employment requirements To be eligible to secure a position, you must either be an Australian citizen , or If you have an Australian government issued visa, you must have Australian residence status (for tax purposes) that allows you to reside and work in Australia indefinitely. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Check, WWCC and a Medical examination How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11:59pm Sunday 7 June 2026. All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Bill Seremetis on 0447 307 880 during business hours. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Senior Place and Design Officer (15095) Requisition ID 15095 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Deliver meaningful public realm and other projects across one of Melbourne’s most liveable municipalities Ongoing, full time, opportunity based in Camberwell with flexible work arrangements $101,985 to $110,964 (plus super and monthly RDO) About Boroondara The City of Boroondara is recognised for its vibrant neighbourhoods, heritage character and high-quality public spaces. Our vision is to be a thriving, inclusive city that meets the needs and aspirations of our community. Guided by strong values and a customer-first mindset, we work collaboratively to deliver places and services that enhance liveability for residents, businesses and visitors. About the Role We are seeking a Senior Place and Design Officer to join our Landscape and Design team within the Capital Projects department. In this role, you will contribute to the planning, design and delivery of high-quality public realm projects across shopping centres, streets, parks, playgrounds and open spaces. You will play a key role in shaping places that are functional, sustainable and reflective of Boroondara’s unique character, while balancing community needs and strategic priorities. Key Responsibilities You will lead the development of public realm design solutions from concept through to construction documentation, ensuring designs meet community expectations and industry standards. The role involves managing projects end-to-end, including budgets, timelines and multidisciplinary teams, as well as contributing to strategic planning, policy development and open space master planning. You will work closely with stakeholders to deliver innovative, well-considered outcomes and ensure effective consultation and communication across projects. You will also foster strong partnerships with internal teams, consultants, contractors, businesses and the community, ensuring project outcomes align with Council’s vision while enhancing user experience and satisfaction. About You You are an experienced and creative professional with a background in landscape architecture, urban design or project management. You bring strong expertise in placemaking, public realm design and community consultation, along with demonstrated experience delivering projects from inception to completion. Your strengths include stakeholder engagement, problem solving and the ability to manage competing priorities while maintaining attention to detail. You are a collaborative team player with excellent communication skills and a proactive approach to innovation and continuous improvement. Your passion for creating great public spaces and your commitment to delivering community-focused outcomes will see you succeed in this role. If you’d like to know more about the role, please see the position description. Employment Requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check, and may also require a Working with Children Check and any relevant pre-employment medical assessments as per the specific position description requirements. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply’ button by 11.59pm Monday 8 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Nathan Milesi Coordinator, Landscape & Design on 03 9278 4444 during business hours or nathan.milesi@boroondara.vic.gov.au I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal - we value your input and look forward to our journey ahead. #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Horticultural Specialist (15097) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Horticultural Specialist (15097) Requisition ID 15097 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Cultivate your career while caring for Boroondara’s parks and gardens Full-time, ongoing opportunity located in Kew $71,312 to $76,789 excl super plus 9-day fortnight If you’ve worked in local government, you know that not all Parks & Gardens teams are created equal. About Boroondara At the City of Boroondara, we are committed to creating a sustainable and inclusive community. We are seeking a dedicated Horticultural Specialist to join our Open Space and Trees team. This is an opportunity to help care for some of Melbourne’s most valued parks, gardens and streetscapes while contributing to a high‐performing and customer‐focused organisation. About the role In this role, you will support the development, care and ongoing maintenance of Council’s parks, formal gardens, streetscapes, carparks and open spaces. You will apply best‐practice horticultural techniques to ensure our green spaces remain healthy, attractive and welcoming for residents and visitors. The role involves working collaboratively with colleagues across Council and engaging respectfully with contractors, community representatives and members of the public. Key responsibilities You will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing horticultural assets using high‐quality horticultural practices. You will regularly assess and prioritise work across multiple sites and ensure all areas meet required standards and maintenance schedules. The role includes monitoring and operating irrigation systems, conducting site audits, identifying redevelopment and planting opportunities, and maintaining minor plant and equipment. You will also uphold safe work procedures and contribute to creating a safe, well‐managed workplace. About you Here, you’ll be entrusted with your own geographic patch – a chance to take ownership, make decisions, and see your work come to life. You’ll be supported by a team that values skill, initiative, and collaboration, and you’ll enjoy the satisfaction of working on some of the most prestigious gardens in the country. This is your opportunity to work in a team that’s proud of what it does and where your contribution will be visible every day – in gardens that are the envy of Melbourne. Want to know more? Please refer to the position description for more information. Employment requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check, a Working with Children check and relevant pre-employment medical assessments How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11.59pm, Sunday 7 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Simon Jones , Acting Coordinator Parks, Environmental Sustainability & Open Spaces on 0466 544 866 during business hours or simon.woods@boroondara.vic.gov.au I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal - we value your input and look forward to our journey ahead. #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the closing_text: Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Local Laws Officer (15080) Requisition ID 15080 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Local Laws Officer Join a dynamic, investigations team that supports each other to succeed Deliver meaningful outcomes to the community in safety and amenity $91,334 - $98,476 per annum (plus Super) Tool of trade vehicle provided (non-hybrid position) Join us in building a professional investigations team and help deliver a responsive, community-focused Local Laws service. In this role, you will contribute to a safe, liveable and well-maintained municipality while providing high-quality regulatory service to residents and stakeholders. About the role At City of Boroondara, our Local Laws directly contributes to our purpose of protecting the health, safety, amenity and assets of the municipality. Based within the large and busy department of Civic Services, the team works as part of five field-based teams and many others within the Urban Living Directorate. You will respond to reported breaches of the Amenity Local Law and the Environment Protection Act, using education, investigation and enforcement to achieve compliance. The role involves managing a varied caseload, gathering evidence, preparing clear records and working within established procedures to resolve matters fairly and effectively. The position includes investigating issues such as illegally dumped rubbish under the EPA, unsightly properties, overhanging vegetation, illegally advertising and the occupation of Council land, while providing courteous and timely service to community members. From time to time, you will also support the Animal Management team with park patrols, and work with external stakeholders including Victoria Police, the Magistrates’ Court and Council prosecutors. Hours are allocated over a 16-week roster (42-hours per week) and rotating weekend work is required every 4 weeks with penalty rates that convert into an annualised salary. A fully maintained vehicle is provided as a ‘tool of trade’ to perform necessary duties and commute to and from work. This is a non-hybrid role as our ‘office’ is in the community, every day and our success comes from working together with other officers, as a team! About you We are looking for someone who brings sound judgement, a strong customer focus and a collaborative approach. You will be supported to build your knowledge and succeed in the role. You will have experience delivering customer service in a complex regulatory environment or another fast-paced setting, with the ability to communicate clearly, build rapport and respond professionally in a range of situations under pressure. Experience in areas such as retail, hospitality, community services or other enforcement role can provide a strong foundation for success in this role. You will be able to investigate issues methodically, collect information accurately and prepare clear notes and reports. Knowledge of compliance, local laws or related legislation will be well regarded, as will the ability to interpret policy and apply it in practical situations. If you are still developing in these areas, a willingness to learn will be equally valued. Success in this role also requires respectful negotiation skills, sound problem-solving and judgement skills, de-escalating interpersonal situations with members of the community, and the capacity to work across the community each day, including walking for extended periods and assisting with animal handling when required. A qualification in enforcement, local laws, justice or a related field is desirable, but not essential. Want to know more? Please refer to the Position description What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development You will be part of a team that makes a real difference. Working at Boroondara means contributing to creating positive change in the world. Join us in a role where your work matters, and you contribute to meaningful outcomes. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check. All City of Boroondara employees must be fully vaccinated. Appointment of the successful candidate will be subject to provision of evidence of vaccination status or medical exemption prior to commencement. How to Apply Follow these steps, carefully: Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply ’ button by Sunday 31 May 2026 11:59pm . Ensure you address your cover letter to Natasha Curmi and answer the following questions: What about the Local Laws Officer role motivates you? Why is this the next logical step in your career? All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any confidential discussion please contact Natasha Curmi, Team Leader Compliance (during business hours) on 0459 854 662 . We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. With so much going on it’s a great time to join Boroondara! #S-DNI Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Email this job to a friend   The job has been sent to Please provide the commencing $61,425 pa (pro-rata) / $31.09 per hour + Super + Allowances Casual - $38.86 per hour (incl. loading) + Super + Allowances Closing Date: Applications are accepted at all times. We are seeking skilled and experienced professional care workers who love supporting people to live independently in the community. We currently provide services in Mount Alexander Shire and Central Goldfields Shire. This is your opportunity to bring your passion and skills to be part of a professional and dedicated team providing high quality in-home support to frail older people and people with disabilities in our community. The role provides tailored services to help clients live healthy, independent and active lives through assistance with daily tasks and activities. If you have any queries or would like to discuss the role, the key contact is: Sarah Newstead In Home Support Coordinator (03) 5471 1845 Please note these positions require staff to be triple vaccinated (2 doses plus a booster). To apply, please provide a cover letter (no more than 2 pages) outlining why you are interested and the best candidate for this role, and your current resume. Mount Alexander Shire Council is committed to child safety. We have zero tolerance of child abuse. Our robust human resources, recruitment and vetting practices are strictly adhered to during the application and interviewing process. Applicants should be aware that we conduct working with children, police and reference checks to ensure that we are recruiting the right people. Apply now Email this job Back to top Acknowledgement of Country Mount Alexander Shire Council acknowledges that the Traditional Custodians of this land, the Dja Dja Wurrung and Taungurung peoples, proudly survive. We acknowledge their continued practise of custom and their close cultural, spiritual, physical, social, historical and economic relationship with the land and waters that make up their Country, which includes Mount Alexander Shire. school crossing supervisor Listen Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Print Content components School crossing supervisors have a much-valued role in Boroondara. On an average school day in Boroondara, they help 9,266 pedestrians to cross the street while 74,426 motor vehicles use our roads during school crossing times. We are always looking for school crossing supervisors. If you are friendly, reliable and flexible, this role is for you. What does a school crossing supervisor do? This paid position gives you the opportunity to get involved in your community and improve road safety for children, people with a disability and the general public who use our 114 school crossings. School crossing supervisors: • work part-time Monday to Friday, from 8 am to 9 am and from 3 pm to 4 pm, during the school term • play an important role in educating children with ‘Stop, Look and Listen’ instructions • are responsible for reporting any road hazards or potential risks • help to encourage active and independent travel. About you We are looking for people aged 18 and older who: • can attend shifts at short notice • have their own reliable transport • are able to work across several locations • can complete the pre-employment medical and police checks. You will have access to a full training program before starting your role. Download the position description School Crossing Supervisor PD 144.04 KB [PDF] 23 November 2022 How to apply Learn more about this rewarding role by reading the position description above. Submit an online application through our Careers website . Attend an interview – meet with one of our Council Officers at Council, located at 8 Inglesby Road in Camberwell. Provide us with relevant employment documents – including your Working With Children Check, Police Check and evidence of your COVID-19 vaccination status. Complete a medical assessment. Apply to become a School Crossings Supervisor We are always recruiting new school crossing supervisors to help people to cross our roads safely at peak school times. More information Call (03) 9278 4774 or email [email protected] for more information. Careers at Boroondara Learn more about what it's like to work with us. Learn more Was this page helpful? Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Senior Administration Officer (15102) Requisition ID 15102 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Lead the delivery of high-quality administration in a dynamic Council environment Ongoing, full-time opportunity based in Kew, flexible arrangements can be considered $84,770 - $98,205 (plus super and monthly RDO) About Boroondara The City of Boroondara is a vibrant, inclusive and thriving community in Melbourne’s inner east. Guided by our vision of a healthy, connected and sustainable community, we deliver services that place our customers at the centre of everything we do. Our people are collaborative, innovative and committed to making a difference for our community every day. About the Role We are seeking a highly organised and proactive Senior Administration Officer to provide professional and high-level support to the Manager Open Space and Trees and the whole department within the Places & Spaces Directorate. Based at Kew Depot, this role plays a key part in ensuring the smooth and effective operation of the department, while acting as a central coordination point for councillor and CEO correspondence. Working across a diverse range of stakeholders, you will deliver quality administrative support, coordinate key processes, and contribute to strong customer service outcomes that support our community and our Department purpose for a greener Boroondara. Key Responsibilities In this role, you will provide comprehensive administrative and organisational support to the Manager and Department, including document preparation, meeting coordination and office management activities. You will manage incoming and outgoing correspondence, ensuring responses are timely and accurate. You will support financial and purchasing processes, including raising purchase orders and maintaining departmental supplies, while ensuring adherence to Council procedures. The role also oversees records management, including monitoring systems and ensuring service standards and response timeframes are achieved. You will deliver high-quality customer service to a wide range of internal and external stakeholders and assist with resolving complex enquiries. Additionally, you will contribute to reporting, process improvements and documentation, maintaining efficient systems that support the department’s performance and compliance obligations. About You You are an experienced administration professional with a strong track record of supporting senior leaders in a complex organisation. You bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to prepare high-quality reports, correspondence and documentation. You are highly organised, detail-oriented and able to prioritise competing demands while meeting deadlines. Your advanced Microsoft Office skills and ability to learn new systems quickly enable you to work efficiently and effectively. With a proactive, solutions-focused mindset, you demonstrate initiative and sound judgement, particularly when dealing with sensitive matters. You thrive both independently and as part of a team, building strong relationships across all levels and delivering exceptional customer service. Please refer to the attached position description for more information. Employment Requirements Applicants must hold unrestricted working rights in Australia, and those progressing through the selection process will need to complete a National Police Record Check and may also require a Working with Children Check. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by 11.59pm, Sunday 14 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager or from recruitment agencies will be considered. For a confidential conversation please contact David Shepard , Manager, Open Space and Trees on 0409 913 482 or david.shepard@boroondara.vic.gov.au during business hours. I f you wa nt to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our careers website boroondara.vic.gov.au/careers The City of Boroondara is committed to providing systems that are accessible to as many people as possible. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some positions may require the incumbent to hold a Working with Children Check. Working together We are currently undertaking an exciting project that will see a transformation in the way we work and the way we serve our customers. Implementation of the Transforming Boroondara Program will provide a customer centric model of service delivery that includes technological innovation, process improvement and business system renewal description_excerpt: Lead the delivery of high-quality administration in a dynamic Council environment Ongoing, full-time opportunity based in Kew, flexible arrangements can be considered $84,770 - $98,205 (plus super and monthly RDO) About Boroondara The City of Boroondara is a vibrant, inclusive and thriving...
title: Senior Administration Officer

advertised_salary_text: $84,770 - $98,205 per annum plus super and RDO We are seeking a capable and community-minded Insurance Offi
closing_text: Career Opportunities: Insurance Officer (15105) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Insurance Officer (15105) Requisition ID 15105 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Insurance Officer Join Council’s Governance and Legal Department Be part of a supportive and collaborative team Full-time, ongoing position $84,770 - $98,205 per annum plus super and RDO We are seeking a capable and community-minded Insurance Officer to join Council’s Governance and Legal Department. In this role, you will manage a broad range of insurance claims, contribute to risk and assurance activities, and help deliver fair, timely and well-informed outcomes for Council and the community. About you We are looking for someone who is passionate about providing outstanding service and making a positive impact in the community. You will bring: A strong understanding of insurance claims management and tort law principles, ideally within a government setting Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to write clear and accurate responses Strong attention to detail, helping you process claims thoroughly and on time A customer-focused approach and a collaborative mindset – you enjoy working with others to achieve the best outcomes Confidence using multiple systems and tools to manage your work efficiently Key responsibilities The primary role of the Insurance Officer is to process claims made against Council. Claims can include damage from Council trees and trucks, personal injury, water flow, and professional liability. You will investigate incidents, draft correspondence, and advocate for outcomes in the best interest of Council and the community. You will also manage some court claims, assisting Council’s insurer and their lawyers. Additionally, you will coordinate the discovery process for litigated claims and manage Freedom of Information requests. As part of the Risk and Assurance team within Council’s Governance and Legal Department, the role also assists with: Crisis management coordination Maintaining Council’s Business Continuity Plans Implementing and managing Council's Risk Management Framework and enterprise risk register Council’s insurance renewal program Fraud and corruption control What sets us apart? An investment in your ongoing professional and personal development. A supportive and friendly workplace culture that values diversity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We offer many staff benefits such as flexible working arrangements, study support, wellbeing programs, gym and pool memberships. Employment requirements Applicants must have the right to work in Australia Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check How to apply Please submit your application online via the ‘Apply’ button by Sunday 7 June 2026, 11:59pm . All applications must be made via the online portal. For further information, please view the position description . For a confidential discussion about this position please contact Rudi Dellow on (03) 9278 4686 during business hours. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state. closing_text: Career Opportunities: Volunteer Sector Development Officer (15092) JavaScript is turned off in your web browser. Turn it on to take full advantage of this site, then refresh the page. Loading... Skip to Main Content Career Opportunities: Volunteer Sector Development Officer (15092) Requisition ID 15092 - Posted - Job Function (1) - Tenure Status (1) - Employment Status (1)  Job Description Print Preview Apply Save Job Email Job to Friend Return to List Volunteer Sector Development Officer Play a key role to promote and raise the profile of volunteering across Boroondara Full time, Fixed Term Contract until 30 June 2027. $84,770 to $98,205 Band 5 per annum plus Superannuation and RDO Make a difference in the community by developing activities that raise the profile of volunteering, develop good volunteer engagement practice, and support community strengthening, social inclusion and wellbeing objectives across Boroondara. A little bit about the role Working in a hybrid environment and reporting to the Volunteer Support & Development Lead, the Volunteer Sector Development Officer will be responsible for a portfolio of activities for which volunteers, volunteer-involving organisations, and Council departments are the primary audience or customer group. The role will also be responsible for, but not limited to: Working in collaboration with the Volunteer Support & Development Lead to develop initiatives which respond to current and emerging trends in volunteering Organising large events designed to connect prospective volunteers including professionals to community organisations as well as recognise and promote volunteering Building the capacity of volunteer-involving organisations, and Council departments to engage volunteers by organising workshops, forums or online resources Supporting internal Council volunteer management Ongoing development of the Boroondara Volunteer Skills Bank, and Ongoing development of the Boroondara volunteer management database A little bit about you You will have outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal, who is both personable and approachable to a range of audiences including all levels of local government, external organisations, networks, and the community. You will have a demonstrated understanding of the volunteer sector. You will have experience in project development and the delivery of large events. You will also have comprehensive computer skills across the Microsoft Office range of applications and a willingness to work as part of a team, prepared to share experiences with other team members to improve overall knowledge. The successful incumbent will also have: a qualification or continuing study in the fields of community development, social science or human services preferred a commitment to quality outcomes and efficient work practices, and excellent time management and organisation skills Employment Requirements Preferred applicants will be required to undergo a National Police Record Check and hold a valid Working with Children Check. How to Apply Please submit your application online via the ‘ Apply’ button by Wednesday 10 June 2026 . All applications must be made via the online portal; no applications direct to the hiring manager will be considered. No calls or applications from agencies please. For any further information please refer to the position description , or contact Tara Cantwell (Acting Volunteer Support and Development Lead) on 9278 4763 or Caddy Purdy (Senior Coordinator Community Strengthening) on 9278 4869 during business hours for a confidential discussion. If you want to know more about the benefits of working for the City of Boroondara, please visit our Careers Website. Council recognises the benefits of a diverse workforce for its employees and customers. To ensure equity of access to employment opportunities, Council encourages applications from underrepresented groups including people who are from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people from different ages, abilities, gender identities, sexual orientation and cultural backgrounds. If you require any additional support or adjustments regarding interview arrangements, please advise the contact listed above in advance. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Council is a child safe organisation and as such, some applicants may be required to hold a Working with Children Clearance. Additionally, some applicants may also be required to undergo a National Criminal History check and/or medical check as per the requirements of the role. We understand that this portal may not meet the needs of people who rely on Assistive Technology or have other diverse requirements. If you have any difficulty using our recruitment system, please do not hesitate to contact People Culture and Development on (03) 9278 4401 or pcd@boroondara.vic.gov.au to allow us to help in any way we can. Who are we? The City of Boroondara is a metropolitan council, representing more than 170,000 people in the inner-eastern suburbs of Melbourne. A leader in the local government sector, with a reputation for innovation and excellence, we believe in providing an environment where our staff thrive, both in the work they do and in how they learn and develop as people and professionals. We like to challenge the norm and we are investing significantly in innovation and technology improvements in order to provide our customers with an even greater experience and engagement. We are putting our customers at the forefront of what we do with our organisation wide initiative, Transforming Boroondara Program. You will be instrumental in driving this change across the organisation using best practice approaches to successfully support us from the now to our desired future state.
